dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. The village is falling apart. People are turning. Tell me you know something.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: I hear a lot of things in here. Most of it's noise. But Sarah, the scientist up at the lab, was working on something. A cure.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Was?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: She hasn't come down in days. Word is she's sick. The lab's locked tight and the power's been out since the grid went down.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Where do I find the key?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Old Doc Whitaker had it on him when he passed. They buried him out at the graveyard. Not a pleasant errand, but it's where you'll find it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And the power?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Talk to George at the powerstation. He'll know what the grid needs. Quietly, mind you — Viktor's people are watching the roads.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. I need the laboratory back on the grid.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Blown fuse. Burned out the night it all started.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Got a spare?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Last one's inside the lab itself. Sarah kept it on a shelf by the door. Get it out, bring it back here, I'll route the line.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'll need the lab key first. It's at the graveyard.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Then start there. I'll be here when you're ready.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah. Can you hear me? It's me.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Stay back. I'm... showing symptoms. Tremors started yesterday. Cognition is slipping faster than I projected.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne said you had a cure.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: A sample. Stabilized. It's in the hospital cold storage — second floor. I moved it before I got worse. The synthesizer here can finish the work, but the power is dead.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: There's a fuse here, isn't there?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: By the door. Take it. George knows what to do. Bring the sample back, synthesize it on the machine, and... please, hurry. I don't have long.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Got the fuse.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Good. Hand it here — no, fit it yourself. Panel's open. I'll talk you through it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Where's the line for the lab?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Third breaker from the left. Slot it in, flip it. Lab gets juice.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Hold it. Identify yourself.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah sent me. There's a cure sample in the cold storage here. I need it.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Scientist. Yeah. She stashed it before she went up the hill. It's still there.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You holding this floor alone?

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: For now. Take what you came for. Don't break it. A lot of people are counting on that little vial, whether they know it or not.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Understood.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I've got the sample. Power's back.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: The synthesizer — left side, the chamber slides open. Load the sample, close it, run the sequence. It will produce a single dose.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And then?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Then you use it. On me. If this works, we'll have the protocol. If it doesn't... at least we'll know.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: It'll work.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: I appreciate the optimism. Please proceed.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah. Something about this outbreak doesn't add up. Too coordinated. Too convenient.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: I've been thinking the same. The strain didn't evolve naturally — the protein folds are too clean. Someone engineered it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Engineered? You're sure?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: As sure as I can be without the source notes. And those notes aren't here. They were taken before I arrived.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Taken where?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: If I had to guess — the outpost. Viktor's people have been making quiet trips through the village for weeks. But there's someone with him. A researcher. Cold. Precise. I overheard the name once — they call him The Doctor.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then he's the one pulling the strings.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Viktor commands the men. But this — the virus, the experiments — that's not Viktor's mind at work. Find The Doctor. Find his records. Then we'll know what we're really fighting.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. I need to know what you've heard about the outpost. The man called The Doctor.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: That name. I was wondering when someone would come asking.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: What can you tell me?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Viktor's men come in here for supplies. They talk less than they think they do. The Doctor doesn't run the outpost — he runs Viktor. Quietly. Viktor doesn't even see it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I need to get to him. The outpost is locked.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: A boy from the village hid a key in the cave before the patrols started. Outpost door, lower levels. He never came back for it. It should still be there.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Thank you, Anne.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Don't thank me yet. If The Doctor knows you're coming, he'll have already calculated three ways to disappear. Move fast.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. I'm headed to the outpost. Anything I should know before I go?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: The lower levels run on a separate line. Independent power. Not mine.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Meaning?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Meaning whoever's down there wanted no one tampering with it. That tells you what they're hiding is worth hiding.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Understood. Stay safe.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Grid stays up. That's my job. Yours is harder.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Marcus. I'm going into the outpost. After The Doctor.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Alone?

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Faster that way. Less noise.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Viktor will be on the second level. Holds court there. The Doctor stays deep — third level, lower workspace. He won't fight you. He'll talk.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: What do I do with him?

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Get what he knows. Then decide. A man like that, the worst punishment is being made irrelevant. Take his work. Walk out.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Copy that.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Move quiet. Past Viktor — don't engage. You won't win that fight today.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: You walked into my house. That takes either courage or stupidity. I haven't decided which.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'm not here for you, Viktor.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: Everyone is here for me eventually. It's the nature of the position.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'm here for the man behind you. The one whispering in your ear.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: The Doctor serves a purpose. He answers to me.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Does he? Or do you just think he does?

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: Walk away while I'm still in a generous mood. Or go ask him yourself — and learn how little you actually understand.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: That's exactly what I'm going to do.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: Ah. The variable arrives. I wondered how long it would take.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You made the virus.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: I refined it. The pathogen existed in a crude form. I gave it elegance. Predictability. A purpose.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Purpose. People are dying.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: People die regardless. What I have done will outlast all of them. Including me. Including you.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Viktor thinks he runs this place. He doesn't, does he.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: Viktor is a useful instrument. He provides walls, men, supplies — and the illusion of command. I provide direction. The arrangement works.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Not anymore. Where are your notes? The original sample?

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: On the bench. The toolkit beside them was meant for the next phase. Take them. I have copies in my head — and that, you cannot take.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Mary. I've got a toolkit. And wood.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: About time someone showed up with both. Hand them over and stand clear.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'll do the work. You walk me through it.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Fine. Hull first, then the engine housing. Pay attention — I'm not saying it twice.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: The boat's our way out. For anyone who wants it.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: That's the idea. Get it floating, and I'll get people off this rock.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Mary. The boat — is she ready?

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: She floats. She runs. That's more than most things on this island can say.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then let's get out of here.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Not so fast. I'm not casting off into open water with empty holds. We need food for the crossing, and I'm not leaving anyone behind who's worth taking.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: What do you need from me?

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Food from Linda's farm. And spread the word — anyone wants out, they meet us here. Quietly. Viktor catches wind of this, we're sunk before we leave the harbour.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Understood. I'll be back.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: See that you are. Tide won't wait.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: You're not here for the view.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Mary's leaving. Taking the boat out, taking anyone who wants out with her. We need food for the crossing.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Figured this day was coming. Crates by the barn. Take what you need.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You're not coming?

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: This is my land. Outbreak or not. I'll outlast it the way I outlast everything else — quietly.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Thank you, Linda.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Just go. And mind the road.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: You've got the look of someone with news.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Mary's boat is fixed. We're leaving tonight. Anyone who wants off the island needs to know.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Quietly, I take it. Viktor has eyes everywhere.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Quietly. Can you pass the word to the ones you trust?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: I can. George at the powergrid will want to know. Sarah in the lab too — she's been waiting for a way out for a long time, even if she'd never say so.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anything I should bring back for you?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Just bring yourself back. The rest will sort itself out.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. Mary's leaving on the boat. There's room for you.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Grid's still running.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: It'll run without you for a night. Viktor won't leave anyone standing once he figures out what's happening.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: ...Fair point. I'll shut the breakers down clean. Head to the dock when I'm done.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Good. Sarah next.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Tell her the lab can burn for all I care. Get her on that boat.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah. It's time. Mary's boat is ready and we're leaving the island.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: I'm close. Days, maybe. If I leave now—

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: If you stay, the Doctor finishes his work before you finish yours. And Viktor decides who lives. Bring your research. Finish it where it's safe.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: ...A rational argument. I'll gather my notes and samples. The mainland will have what I need to continue. Properly.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Meet at the dock. Quiet as you can.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Understood. Thank you. Truly.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: You shouldn't be up here.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Mary's boat sails tonight. There's a seat for you.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Negative. Someone holds the line, the rest of you get clear. That's how this works.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Viktor will come for the dock the moment he hears the engine. I need you between him and us.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Now that's an order I can work with. I'll take the village road. He'll have to come through me.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'll fortify the hospital before I go. Fall back here if it turns.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Understood. Move.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Word came down. George shut the grid. Sarah's already aboard with her crates. You bring the food?

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Everything Linda could spare. Marcus is holding the village road. Linda and Anne are staying — their choice.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Then we cast off. Get aboard.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Viktor?

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Let him shout at the wake. By the time he reaches the dock we'll be past the breakwater.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then take us out, Captain.
